article_title: Critical FortiClient EMS SQL Injection Under Active Exploitation as CISA Deadline Hits Tomorrow

content_preview.title: Critical FortiClient EMS SQL Injection Under Active Exploitation as CISA Deadline Hits Tomorrow
content_preview.summary: A pre-authentication SQL injection in Fortinet's endpoint management server has been exploited since late March, with federal agencies facing an April 16 remediation deadline.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A critical SQL injection vulnerability in Fortinet's FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) is under active exploitation, and the deadline for U.S. federal agencies to patch or mitigate the flaw arrives on April 16. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-21643 with a CVSS score of 9.8, allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands and potentially gain full control of the underlying server through specially crafted HTTP requests.

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong infosec article on FortiClient EMS CVE-2026-21643 active exploitation.
editor_notes.source_verification: Bishop Fox VERIFIED via WebFetch: CVE-2026-21643, pre-auth SQL injection via Site header, FortiClient EMS 7.4.4, multi-tenant mode, format-string root cause, 7.4.5 patch all confirmed. CISA KEV and April 16 deadline from secondary sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Technical root cause verified. Minor CVSS discrepancy (9.1 Bishop Fox vs 9.8 article).
editor_notes.originality: No prior FortiClient EMS CVE coverage.
